data.title: Cboe Global Markets Announces Major Restructuring and Cost Cuts
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Cboe Global Markets ( (CBOE) ).

On May 1, 2026, Cboe Global Markets, Inc., a major operator of cash and spot markets, derivatives exchanges, and market data services under its Cboe Data Vantage unit, reported record first-quarter 2026 financial results, including a 29% rise in net revenue to $728.9 million and a 54% jump in diluted EPS to $3.66. Strong growth in cash and spot markets, a 32% increase in derivatives revenues on record index options volumes, and a 19% gain in Data Vantage revenues underpinned an expansion in operating and EBITDA margins.

Alongside the earnings, Cboe advanced its strategic realignment launched after a 2025 portfolio review, raising 2026 organic net revenue and Data Vantage growth targets while cutting adjusted operating expense guidance to $838–$853 million. The company announced further restructuring measures expected to reduce its workforce by about 20% when combined with earlier asset sales and wind-downs, incurring $36–$46 million in pre-tax charges largely for severance from the second to fourth quarters of 2026, and targeting $40–$50 million in annualized pre-tax cost savings to fund investment in core franchises and emerging areas such as financial event markets, tokenization, expanded clearing, and global sales and education efforts.

The most recent analyst rating on (CBOE) stock is a Hold with a $330.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cboe Global Markets stock, see the CBOE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CBOE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CBOE is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (expanding margins, robust cash generation, and improving leverage). Technicals also support the rating with a clear uptrend, though momentum is getting stretched. Valuation is a mild headwind due to a moderate P/E and low dividend yield, while the earnings call adds support but is tempered by slower 2026 growth guidance and strategic/competitive uncertainties.

To see Spark’s full report on CBOE stock, click here.

More about Cboe Global Markets

Cboe Global Markets, Inc. is a Chicago-based exchange operator focused on cash and spot markets, derivatives, and market data services through its Cboe Data Vantage business. The company generates revenue from trading in index options and other products, clearing services in the U.S. and Europe, and data and access solutions for a global client base of investors and trading firms.

On May 1, 2026, Cboe reported record first-quarter 2026 results, with net revenue up 29% to $728.9 million and diluted EPS up 54% to $3.66, driven by strong performance in cash and spot markets, derivatives, and a 19% revenue gain in Data Vantage. The company raised its 2026 organic net revenue growth and Data Vantage growth targets, cut adjusted operating expense guidance, and detailed the next phase of a strategic realignment that began after a 2025 review, including actions expected to reduce its workforce by about 20%, incur $36–$46 million in restructuring charges through 2026, and deliver $40–$50 million in annualized cost savings while reallocating investment toward core and emerging growth areas.

Average Trading Volume: 896,615

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Schneider National Shareholders Approve Incentive Plan and Governance Changes
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Schneider National ( (SNDR) ) has issued an update.

On April 30, 2026, Schneider National’s shareholders approved an amendment and restatement of the 2017 Omnibus Incentive Plan, increasing the Class B common stock reserved for issuance under the plan by 19,900,000 shares, which expands the company’s capacity to grant equity-based awards and may further align management and employee incentives with long-term shareholder interests. The Board also adopted amended and restated bylaws that enhance the role of the lead independent director in calling special board meetings and chairing stockholder and board sessions when the board chair is unavailable, a move that could strengthen governance flexibility and continuity.

At the April 30, 2026 annual meeting, shareholders elected ten directors to serve until the next annual meeting, ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as independent auditor for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2026, and supported the amended incentive compensation plan, with approximately 97.5% of eligible votes represented. Investors also gave advisory approval to the compensation of the company’s named executive officers, signaling broad shareholder support for the current leadership, incentive structure, and oversight framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (SNDR) stock is a Hold with a $33.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Schneider National stock, see the SNDR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SNDR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SNDR is a Neutral.

The score is driven by solid financial resilience (low leverage and improved cash generation) but offset by weak current earnings power (lower margins/returns and negative TTM revenue growth). Technically, the trend is positive yet near-term momentum appears overbought. Valuation is a meaningful headwind given the high P/E and modest yield, while the latest earnings call supports a cautious recovery narrative via maintained guidance and cost/productivity initiatives amid demand risk.

To see Spark’s full report on SNDR stock, click here.

The company operates a multi-segment business model that combines asset-based trucking with non-asset logistics offerings to serve a broad customer base and manage complex supply chains.

Douglas Dynamics ( (PLOW) ) has issued an update.

On April 30, 2026, Douglas Dynamics amended its Fourth Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation in Delaware to add exculpation provisions that limit personal liability for certain officers, following stockholder approval of the change at its annual meeting on April 29, 2026. At that 2026 annual meeting, shareholders elected four directors to terms expiring in 2028 and 2029, approved the company’s executive compensation on an advisory basis, ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as independent auditor for 2026, and backed the officer exculpation amendment, signaling broad investor support for the board’s governance and risk-allocation framework.

These governance changes may strengthen Douglas Dynamics’ ability to attract and retain senior executives by narrowing certain legal exposures while keeping oversight with shareholders through regular director elections. The strong affirmative vote on pay practices and auditor ratification further reinforces continuity in the company’s leadership, compensation design, and financial reporting oversight, which are key concerns for institutional investors and other long-term stakeholders.

On May 1, 2026, Stantec Inc. filed a Form 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, reaffirming its governance framework as a foreign private issuer reporting under Form 40-F. data.title: RREEF Property Trust Announces April 2026 Monthly Distributions
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

RREEF Property Trust, Inc. Class A ( (ZRPTAX) ) has provided an announcement.

RREEF Property Trust, Inc. declared monthly distributions on May 1, 2026 for all classes of its common stock for the month of April 2026, with payments scheduled for May 4, 2026 to stockholders of record as of May 1, 2026. The company set a uniform gross distribution of $0.07495 per share across all classes, but net distributions vary by share class due to differing dealer manager and distribution fees, resulting in higher net payouts for classes without such fees.

Class A investors will receive a net distribution of $0.06380 per share, while Class I shareholders will get $0.06928 and Class T and Class T2 will receive $0.06211 and $0.06562 respectively, reflecting their specific fee structures. Holders of Classes D, M-I, N, and Z, which carry no dealer manager or distribution fees, will receive the full $0.07495 per share, underscoring how share class selection directly affects investor income from the trust’s distributions.

More about RREEF Property Trust, Inc. Class A

RREEF Property Trust, Inc. is a real estate investment trust that offers multiple classes of common stock, providing individual and institutional investors with access to diversified real estate investment strategies. The company structures share classes with varying fee arrangements, influencing net distributions received by different categories of shareholders.

For an in-depth examination of ZRPTAX stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

IES Holdings ( (IESC) ) has shared an announcement.

IES Holdings reported strong results for its fiscal second quarter ended March 31, 2026, with revenue up 17% year over year to $974 million and operating income up 21% to $112.3 million. Net income jumped 56% to $109.9 million, while remaining performance obligations reached about $2.3 billion and backlog climbed to roughly $3.9 billion, reflecting accelerating customer demand.

Growth was driven by robust performance in the Communications and Infrastructure Solutions segments, particularly in data center and industrial markets, while the Residential business weakened on soft housing starts and pricing pressure. The January 2026 acquisition of Gulf Island Fabrication boosted Infrastructure Solutions revenue and underpins a strategy of prioritizing capacity expansion and organic growth, with management significantly increasing capital spending plans despite expecting Gulf Island’s earnings contribution to be more meaningful in fiscal 2027.

The Commercial & Industrial segment delivered modest revenue growth but significantly higher operating income on strong project execution and data center demand. During the quarter, IES deployed capital toward the Gulf Island purchase, capex, marketable securities, and share repurchases, yet finished with net cash and sizable liquidity, positioning the company to support its expanding backlog and multi-year growth initiatives.

Spark’s Take on IESC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, IESC is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (material margin improvement, high ROE, and modest leverage) and constructive technical momentum (price above key moving averages with positive MACD). Valuation is only moderate (P/E ~21) and cash flow conversion/free cash flow momentum is a notable watch item, while recent corporate actions are net positive.

data.title: Colgate-Palmolive Expands Strategic Growth and Restructuring Program
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Colgate-Palmolive ( (CL) ).

On July 31, 2025, Colgate-Palmolive’s board approved a three-year Strategic Growth and Productivity Program aimed at aligning its organizational structure with its 2030 strategy, optimizing its global supply chain and reducing overhead costs. Building on progress to date, the board expanded the program on April 30, 2026, significantly increasing its scope and associated restructuring charges.

The program is now expected to generate cumulative pre-tax charges of $350 million to $550 million, up from a prior estimate of $200 million to $300 million, with 70% to 80% tied to severance and other employee-related costs and 20% to 30% to asset write-offs, accelerated depreciation and contract exits. The charges, 80% to 90% of which will require cash outlays, are expected to be largely incurred by December 31, 2028, with impacts spread across all major regions, Hill’s Pet Nutrition and corporate functions, signaling a broad-based reshaping of Colgate-Palmolive’s operations to underpin future growth and margin improvement.

The most recent analyst rating on (CL) stock is a Hold with a $79.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Colgate-Palmolive stock, see the CL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CL is a Neutral.

The score is driven mainly by strong cash-flow generation and a durable revenue base, partially offset by elevated balance-sheet risk (high leverage vs. very thin equity). Technical indicators are weak and weigh on the rating, while valuation is mixed (reasonable yield but negative P/E). The earnings call supports steady execution (Hill’s/emerging markets strength) but highlights meaningful near-term uncertainty and a wide guidance range.

To see Spark’s full report on CL stock, click here.

More about Colgate-Palmolive

Colgate-Palmolive is a global consumer products company focused on household, oral care, personal care and pet nutrition categories, marketed under brands including Hill’s Pet Nutrition. The company operates across major regions including North America, Latin America, Europe, the Middle East & Africa and Asia Pacific, and is pursuing a long-term 2030 growth strategy centered on efficiency and agility.

data.title: NPK International Highlights Growth in May 2026 Presentation
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from NPK International ( (NPKI) ).

NPK International Inc. operates as a specialty rental and services company focused on temporary worksite access solutions for power and infrastructure markets. It manufactures, sells, and rents recyclable composite matting products, led by its DURA-BASE advanced-composite mat system, and supports customers with planning, logistics, installation, and site restoration across a global footprint.

The company is vertically integrated with engineering, manufacturing in Carencro, La., and a large rental fleet of about 220,000 composite mats serving diverse end-markets, including power transmission, oil and gas, pipeline, infrastructure construction, and rail. It reported trailing 12‑month revenue through the first quarter of 2026 of $287 million and adjusted EBITDA of $78 million, with approximately 69% of revenue from rental and services and 31% from product sales.

NPK’s May 2026 investor presentation highlighted continued growth, with trailing 12‑month revenue up 23% year over year and adjusted EBITDA up 26%, translating into an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.3% and modest net debt of $4 million as of March 31, 2026. Management attributed performance to strong demand for its composite matting solutions, increased production capacity, and a flexible model that offers both rental and purchase options, positioning the company to benefit from sustained investment in power transmission and other infrastructure projects.

The company emphasized the scale of its U.S. composite mat manufacturing and rental fleet, ongoing capacity expansion, and focus on safety, environmental performance, and lifecycle cost advantages as key differentiators in its markets. Its end‑market mix and the regulated, long‑term nature of power transmission investments provide a stable demand backdrop, while non‑GAAP metrics such as adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow are used internally and by investors to assess operating performance and capital structure.

The most recent analyst rating on (NPKI) stock is a Buy with a $18.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on NPK International stock, see the NPKI Stock Forecast page.

The main offset is valuation (P/E ~27.5 with no dividend yield provided) and ongoing cyclicality/concentration risks highlighted by management.

More about NPK International

1St Source ( (SRCE) ) has issued an update.

1st Source Corporation’s executive team has released an investor presentation for the first quarter of 2026 that highlights the bank’s diversified business mix, growing renewable energy financing platform, and expanding digital capabilities. The materials underscore the company’s long-standing mission to remain an independent, high-service community bank while leveraging specialty finance and national lending niches to drive long-term financial performance.

The presentation details strong activity in renewable energy finance, with financed solar and storage projects estimated to avoid hundreds of thousands of metric tons of carbon emissions annually as of March 31, 2026, reinforcing 1st Source’s role in funding sustainable infrastructure. It also showcases significant uptake in digital banking, including a highly rated mobile app, rising Zelle usage, and rapid growth in real-time and FedNow instant payments since their phased launch in 2023, indicating ongoing modernization of client services and payment capabilities.

Management emphasizes the depth of its leadership bench, with executives and lending officers averaging two to three decades of experience, and highlights recognition such as repeated Indiana SBA Community Lender Gold Awards and leading deposit share in its core markets. These operational strengths and accolades position 1st Source as a prominent regional player with specialized national lending franchises, which is likely to support its competitive standing with business clients, renewable energy developers, and community stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (SRCE) stock is a Hold with a $74.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on 1St Source stock, see the SRCE Stock Forecast page.

To see Spark’s full report on SRCE stock, click here.

More about 1St Source

1st Source Corporation is a $9.1 billion community bank headquartered in South Bend, Indiana, with a national and international reach through its specialty finance and renewable energy businesses. It offers community banking services, including business and personal banking, lending, mortgages, leasing, wealth and trust services, and insurance, alongside renewable energy project financing and specialty finance for autos, trucks, construction equipment, and aircraft.

The bank operates 78 banking centers, 98 ATMs, multiple trust and wealth advisory locations managing about $6.3 billion in assets, and 13 insurance offices across northern Indiana, greater Indianapolis, and southwestern Michigan. Its loan and lease book is nearly evenly split between community banking and specialty finance, and it has built a sizable renewable energy portfolio across the U.S., with over $713 million in loans and leases and more than $207 million in tax equity investments as of March 31, 2026.

data.title: Arvinas Gains First FDA Approval for Breast Cancer Drug
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Arvinas Holding Company ( (ARVN) ) has shared an announcement.

On May 1, 2026, Arvinas and partner Pfizer announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved VEPPANU (vepdegestrant) for adults with ER-positive/HER2-negative, ESR1-mutated advanced or metastatic breast cancer whose disease has progressed after at least one line of endocrine-based therapy. The approval came ahead of the June 5, 2026 PDUFA date and is based on the Phase 3 VERITAC-2 trial, in which VEPPANU significantly improved progression-free survival versus fulvestrant with mostly low-grade adverse events.

VEPPANU is the first and only FDA-approved PROTAC protein degrader, marking Arvinas’s first approved medicine and a milestone for the targeted protein degradation field, while offering a new oral option for patients facing endocrine resistance and limited second-line choices. The decision triggers a $50 million development milestone payment from Pfizer to Arvinas, net of amounts owed to Yale University under a license agreement, and the partners plan to appoint a third-party commercialization partner to maximize VEPPANU’s market potential.

The most recent analyst rating on (ARVN) stock is a Buy with a $18.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Arvinas Holding Company stock, see the ARVN Stock Forecast page.

On April 30, 2026, Amkor Technology priced a private offering of $1 billion in 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2031 to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A, with settlement expected on May 5, 2026 and an option for purchasers to buy up to an additional $150 million. The notes, guaranteed on a senior unsecured basis by key subsidiaries, carry an initial conversion price of about $106.37 per share, a 52.5% premium to the stock’s last sale price, and feature conditional conversion, redemption and repurchase provisions tied to stock performance and fundamental change events.

data.title: PDS Biotechnology Refinances Debt with New Note, Warrant
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

PDS Biotechnology ( (PDSB) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 30, 2026, PDS Biotechnology entered into a securities purchase agreement with YA II PN, LTD. under which it will issue a $6 million promissory note, sold at a discount, and a warrant for up to 2,158,274 common shares at $1.1824 per share, with closing expected around June 12, 2026 subject to customary conditions including continued Nasdaq listing. The one-year note carries 10% interest, is guaranteed by a subsidiary, restricts new indebtedness and variable-rate financings, and is paired with a new at-the-market offering agreement with Yorkville Securities, while proceeds are expected to help redeem existing senior secured convertible debentures on June 12, 2026 and replace a prior at-the-market program terminated on May 1, 2026.

The promissory note includes investor-friendly features such as optional accelerated amortization, conditional conversion at a discount to market if PDS misses payments, and caps on ownership and dilution tied to Nasdaq rules, while the warrant similarly limits exercises to avoid breaching ownership and 19.99% issuance thresholds without shareholder approval. In parallel, PDS plans to register the shares underlying the note and warrant within 30 days of closing and has structured its new at-the-market facility so that cash raised will largely be directed toward servicing and amortizing the new debt, signaling an effort to refinance its capital structure and reduce reliance on its older convertible debentures.

On the effective date of the new agreement, PDS delivered irrevocable redemption notices to prepay in cash all outstanding senior secured convertible debentures issued under an April 30, 2025 financing, at 103% of principal plus accrued interest and other amounts due as of the June 12, 2026 redemption date. The company also terminated its prior at-the-market issuance sales agreement with B. Riley Securities and H.C. Wainwright on May 1, 2026, consolidating its equity capital-raising activities under the new arrangement with Yorkville and reworking its balance sheet to reduce legacy secured convertible debt exposure.
